qText: A bookshop advertises: "Buy 3 books and get the 4th at 40% discount." If the marked price of each book is Rs. 500 and all books are priced the same, what is the effective percentage discount the customer receives on the total purchase?

A high-end watch is initially marked up by 60% above its cost price. During a sale, it is offered at a discount of 25% on its marked price. However, a special customer receives an additional discount of y% on the discounted price, bringing the final selling price to ‚Çπ6480. If the shopkeeper still makes a profit of 8% on the cost price after all discounts, what was the original cost price of the watch and the value of y ?

A furniture manufacturer sells a dining table set to a showroom at a 20% discount on the marked price, but adds a 10% handling fee on the discounted price. The showroom then sells the set for ‚Çπ3500 more than what they paid, thereby earning a profit of 20%. At what price had the manufacturer marked the dining table set?
A retailer offers a successive discount of 20% and 12% on a TV set marked at Rs. 60,000. What is the final selling price?
A wholesaler offers a 20% discount on purchases of ‚Çπ20,000 or more. A customer buys goods worth ‚Çπ25,000. If he also gets an additional 5% discount for paying cash, how much does he pay?

A banner at a garment shop advertises 'Buy 2 and get 5'. The shopkeeper has marked all the shirts at the same amount. Gaurav was his relative and the shopkeeper gave him an additional discount of 2% on the total bill. Gaurav purchased 5 shirts and paid ‚Çπ1,274. What is the marked price of each shirt ?

In a scheme, a shopkeeper offers 4% discount if you buy three or more products. Ritu buys three bottles of guava juice for ‚Çπ330. If the shopkeeper still makes a profit of 10%, find the profit he would make if had sold it without discount (rounded off to 2 decimal places).

A shopkeeper buys two varieties of sugar at ‚Çπ120 and ‚Çπ80 per kg, respectively, and mixes them in the ratio 2 : 3. He then sets the marked price of each kg of the blended sugar at 50% above the cost he incurred per kg of the same, and then sold it after offering a 25% discount on the marked price. At what price (in ‚Çπ) did the shopkeeper sell each kg of the blended sugar ?

Amit and Bilal buy goods for ‚Çπ1,500 and ‚Çπ2,000, respectively. Amit marks his goods up by y%, while Bilal marks his goods up by 2y% and offers a discount of y%. If both make the same profit, then find the value of y.

If Ram purchases a fan, he gets 8% discount, however, if he purchases three fans he gets 6% on the first, 10% on the second and 12% on the third. If the price paid by the Ram for three fans is ‚Çπ6,800, then what will be the marked price of each fan ?

Ramesh purchased some items from a dealer on 19 November 2019. The bill generated was for ‚Çπ5,480. He had also purchased some items on 10 November 2019 having the bill value of ‚Çπ9,800. He cleared both the bills this time and paid the total amount in cash. If the dealer gives a scheme of 2% discount on the payments done within 10 days and 5% for the payments made in cash at the time of purchase, then find the amount paid by Ramesh on 19 November 2019.

What is the discount that Rohan should offer on the remaining ‚Çπ 8,000 of a laptop priced at ‚Çπ 48,000, given that, he has already given a 12% discount on the first ‚Çπ 28,000 and 8% discount on the next ‚Çπ 12,000 to match the discount amount of 9.5% given on the total price?

A seller offers a discount of 12% if the total sale price of different products purchased by a single customer is below ‚Çπ3,000, and discount of 15% if the total sale value of all the products exceeds ‚Çπ3,000. A shirt costing ‚Çπ900 was sold to Anand after earning a margin of 9% and also a jacket costing ‚Çπ1,900 was sold to him after a margin of 6%. How much does Anand have to pay (in ‚Çπ) for the final settlement of the bill ?

A shopkeeper offers 3 schemes of discounts to his customers. Which of the following schemes will fetch the minimum discount percentage ? A) Two successive discounts of 24% and 27%. B) Buy 5 get three free. C) Buy 6 and get 10.
A merchant fixes the sale price of his goods at 42% above the cost price. He sells his goods at a 15% discount on marked price. His percentage of profit (rounded off to the nearest integer) is:

If the price of an item is increased by 70% and then allows a scheme discount of 5% and 20% on this marked price. If price is paid in cash and an additional discount of 20% is allowed then what is his gain percentage ?

On an item having a marked price of 1,600, successive discounts of y% ‚Çπ and z% were offered. As a result, the item was sold for 1,056, Which of the ‚Çπ options below CANNOT be a set of values for y and z ?

A shopkeeper offers the following discount schemes for buyers on an article: I. Two successive discounts of 15% each II. A discount of 25% followed by a discount of 5% III. Two successive discounts of 20% and 10% IV. A discount of 30% Under which scheme will the selling price be maximum ?

An electronic store owner allows two successive discounts of 20% and 25% on each item. The store has a reward points scheme which enables a customer to get free shopping worth ‚Çπ0.10 on every 1 reward point credited to the customer's account on previous purchases from the store. A customer decides to buy a laptop that is marked at ‚Çπ72,000. What will be its net selling price if he has 2850 reward points to his credit

Victoria bought items that had a combined face value of $400,000. She was offered a 10% discount on part of the payment made in cash and a 2% surcharge on the portion of payments made via credit card. If Victoria paid a total of $3,78,000, how much did she pay, including surcharges, via credit card ?

The marked price of every item being sold by a wholesaler was ‚Çπ400. The wholesaler was offering a stock-clearance sale, under which for every four items paid for, one item was being given free. In addition to that, a further 5% discount on the amount payable Son the 'Buy 4, Get 1 free' scheme price was being offered to anyone making purchases worth more than 15,000. Rameshwar made purchases for which the amount payable, before applying the 5% discount, was 16,000. What was the effective discount percentage that was finally offered to Rameshwar during this transaction ?
A trader marked a table at ‚Çπ37,062, and sold it allowing a 33% discount. If his profit was 42%, then the cost price (in ‚Çπ ) of the table was:

A trader offers to sell a commodity under any of the two schemes mentioned below: Scheme 1: The trader offers two successive discounts of 20% and 12.5% on the sale of each unit. Scheme 2: The trader offers the buyer an option to ‚Äòpay for y units, get z units free‚Äô. The buyer realizes that if he needs to buy (y + z) units, it does not matter to him which of the two schemes he opts for. Which of the following options gives a correct ordered pair of (y, z) ?

Raghav went to a shopping mall to purchase clothes. He had two coupons with him, but only one can be used on a single day. Using the first coupon, a total discount of 30% could be obtained on the total amount. Using the second coupon, he will get 80% off on the price of the costliest shirt if he buys at least 3 shirts. What is the price at which Raghav can purchase 3 shirts at a minimum price if the prices of the three shirts are Rs.1,250, Rs.1,540 and Rs.1,375 ?
A shopkeeper offers the following three schemes. Scheme-I: Two successive discounts of 15% and 25%. Scheme-ll: Buy 5, get 3 free. Scheme-III: Buy 4, get 6. Which scheme is the best for customers?
Sunil purchased a fan at a 10% discount on the labelled price. If he had purchased it at a 15% discount, he would have saved ‚Çπ400. Find the labelled price of the fan.

Under a sale offer, Tanvir was offered a 32% discount on the part of the marked price that was paid in cash, but had to add 1.2% on the part of the marked price paid through a credit card. If Tanvir paid 75% of the marked price in cash and the rest through a credit card, what percentage of the marked price was his total final payment ?

The price of a cell phone is ‚Çπ20,000. On Sundays, the shopkeeper offers a cash discount of ‚Çπ1,000 on the purchase of the cell phone. Further, if someone purchases it through a credit card, he gives 5% additional discount. If someone is purchasing the cell phone on a Sunday through a credit card, then how much does he/she have to pay ?
An article was marked at ‚Çπ x and sold at a discount of (x - 40)%. If the customer paid ‚Çπ(x - 32), then find the marked price of the article.

A dealer allows 25% discount on the marked price of an article and gains 20%. If the cost price of the article increases by 20%, what percent discount should he allow on the marked price so that his profit percentage remains the same ?

The marked price of an article is Rs 1500. If two successive discounts, each of x%, on the marked price is equal to a single discount of Rs 587.40, then what will be the selling price of the article if a single discount of x% is given on the marked price ?

A dealer buys an article marked at Rs20000 with two successive discounts of 20% and 5%. He spends Rs1000 for its repair and sells it for Rs 20000. What is his profit/loss percent (correct to two decimal places) ?

There was 25% off on shirts. A lady bought that shirt and got an additional 20% discount for paying in cash and further 10% discount for being a loyal customer. She paid Rs 324. What was the price tag (in Rs) on the shirt ?
By how much above the cost price should an article be marked up for sale so that after allowing two successive discounts of 20% and 6.25% on it, a net gain of 20% is made on the cost ?

A shopkeeper sells an item for Rs 492 after allowing 18% discount on its marked price. Had he not allowed any discount, he would have earned a profit of 20% on the cost price. What is the cost price of the item ?
A dealer buys an article at a discount of 20% on its list price and marks it at 25% above the list price. If he allows a 20% discount on the new list price, then his profit percent is :
A man purchases 100 copies of a book from the publisher and gets a discount of 25%. He buys 50 copies from a retailer at a discount of 10%. He got an overall discount of:
The price of a refrigerator is Rs 22000. A shopkeeper marks its price 15% above its cost price and gives a discount of 8%. The discount is:

A trader gives a discount of 4% for purchases above Rs.25,000, 6% for purchases above Rs.35000 and 8% for purchases above Rs. 50,000. If the price of an item is marked Rs.38,500. What would be the amount of discount ?

A toy manufacturer produced 1200 toy cars at a total cost of ‚Çπ91,000. He donated 200 cars to a charity event. For the rest, he announced a 10% discount on the market price of ‚Çπ120 per car. He also offered 2 toy cars free for every 8 toy cars purchased. If all 1200 toy cars were distributed, what is his overall gain or loss percentage ?

A retailer marks a car 80% above its cost price. He offers a first discount of 25% on the marked price. During a festive offer, an additional discount of 10% is applied on the already discounted price. If the final selling price is ‚Çπ19,440, what is the approximate cost price of the car ?"

A mobile phone retailer sells a phone for ‚ÇπP and makes a profit of 30%. For a special festive offer, he sells the same phone for ‚Çπ1.5P. He offers a 10% discount on the offer. What percentage profit will he make during the festive offer?
A shopkeeper marked an item 20% above its cost price. During a sale, he offered a discount of 10%. What was his profit percentage?

A shopkeeper marks his goods at 50% above the cost price. He allows a discount of 20% on the marked price. If he also gives an additional cash discount of ‚Çπ 300 , and still makes a profit of 14% on the cost price, what is the cost price of the goods?

A wholesaler buys a batch of ceiling fans at a 40% markup over the factory's base cost ‚ÇπP. He then allows a 10% trade discount to the retailer. The retailer applies a 50% markup on his cost and offers a flat 10% discount to customers on the printed price. If a customer finally pays ‚Çπ20412 , what is the original base cost (‚ÇπP) of one ceiling fan from the factory ?

A bookseller sells a novel for ‚Çπ A and makes a loss of 15%. To clear old stock, he decides to mark the novel at ‚Çπ0.8A. He then allows a further discount of 10% on this marked price. What will be the percentage loss that he will incur on this clearance sale?
An article is marked at ‚Çπ600. A shop owner allows a discount of 6% and still gains 4%. What is the approximate cost price of the article ?

A wholesaler marks up the price of a microwave oven by 50 % above its cost price. He gives a 15% trade discount to a retailer. The retailer, in turn, marks up the price by 20 % above his purchase price and offers a 10% festival discount to the customer. If the customer finally pays ‚Çπ12,200 for the microwave, what is the original approximate cost price of the oven to the wholesaler ?
A dress is marked at ‚Çπ4,500. A discount offer changes from 12% to 17%. How much extra discount does a customer get?
A dealer sets the marked price of a television 40% higher than its cost price. If he gives a discount of 25% and makes a profit of ‚Çπ600, what is the cost price of the television ?

A merchant marked an article such that its price was 30% above its cost price. He offered two consecutive discounts of 20% and 12.5% to a buyer. If he incurred a loss of ‚Çπ540, what was the marked price of the article?

A furniture store sells a chair for ‚ÇπZ, making a 20% loss. During a festive season, they increase the marked price of the same chair to ‚Çπ2.6Z. They then offer a special discount of 40% on this increased marked price. What will be the percentage profit made by the store during the festive season?
A mobile shop owner buys 10 boxes of mobiles for the price of 8 boxes from a wholesaler. If he sells each box at a 2% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage?

A fruit seller offers mangoes to a retailer at a 30% discount on the marked price. However, they charge an additional 5% on the discounted price for shipping. The retailer sells the mangoes for ‚Çπ1620 more, earning a profit of 40%. What was the marked price of the mangoes?
A shopkeeper marked an item 80% above its cost price. He then offered two successive discounts of 20% and 25%. If he made a profit of ‚Çπ160, at what price did he sell the item ?
The marked price of a TV is ‚Çπ30,000. Amit receives two successive discounts while buying it. If the first discount is 20% and the final price he pays is ‚Çπ16,800, what is the second discount rate?
A manufacturer marked a product 20% above its cost price. He provided a discount to a customer, which resulted in him making a profit of 8%. What was the rate of discount given by the manufacturer?

A gadget is sold at a 25% discount on its marked price. This sale leads to a profit of 10% on its cost price. If the profit earned is ‚Çπ300, what would have been the selling price if the gadget was sold at a 20% discount on its marked price?
An item costs ‚Çπ300. A customer gets a 20% discount on it. If the seller still makes a 25% profit, what is the cost price of the item?

A web developer sells a premium website template to a distributor at a 25% discount. An additional 10% service fee is added to the discounted price. The distributor then sells the template for ‚Çπ9000 more, earning a 20% profit. What is the original marked price of the template fixed by the developer?
A vendor purchases 5 shirts at the marked price of 4 shirts. If he sells each shirt after giving a 8% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage ?

A refrigerator has a marked price of ‚Çπ 15,000. It is sold with two successive discounts. If the second discount is 30% and the final selling price is ‚Çπ 9,000, what is the percentage of the first discount ?

A furniture store marks its items at 60% above the cost price. They offer a discount of 20% on the marked price. If a customer receives an additional loyalty discount of ‚Çπ221, and the store still makes a 15% profit on the cost price, what is the cost price of the furniture item ?
A shopkeeper marked a mobile phone at ‚Çπ800. He sold it at a 15% discount. If his profit on the sale was 20%, what was the mobile phone's purchase price?

A product is sold at a 20% discount on its marked price. This transaction results in a profit of 8% on its cost price. If the profit earned is ‚Çπ224, what would have been the selling price if the product was sold at a 33.33% discount on its marked price?

A bookseller purchases 6 novels at the marked price of 8 novels from a distributor. If he then sells these novels to customers, offering a discount of 15% on the marked price, what is his profit percentage ?
A pair of shoes is listed at ‚Çπ900. After two successive discounts, it is sold for ‚Çπ612. If the first discount offered was 20%, what was the second discount percentage?
A retailer marked a product 60% above its cost price. He provided two successive discounts of 25% and 10%. If he earned a profit of ‚Çπ240, what was the cost price of the product?

A Phone is priced at ‚Çπ44,000. A customer gets a discount of ‚Çπ3,000 on it. After the discount, the customer buys a charger for ‚Çπ1,600 at a 30% discount. What is the total amount the customer spends on both items?

A book, originally priced at ‚Çπ800, is subject to a reduction in cost through two consecutive discounts: an initial markdown of 30%, followed by an additional 20% discount. The question then is to determine the conclusive selling price of the book once both these successive price adjustments have been applied.

Having initially established the marked price of a particular commodity at a 25% premium over its cost price, a retail vendor subsequently extended an unspecified discount. If, following this concession, the transaction ultimately yielded a profit of 20% on the cost price, what was the exact percentage of the discount given ?
A trader marked an article 30% above its cost price. He then allowed a discount of 10% on the marked price. If the selling price of the article was ‚Çπ702, what was its cost price ?

A furniture establishment initially disposes of a particular dining table for ‚ÇπX, thereby realising a 20% profit margin. Subsequently, as a promotional strategy for a festive period, the marked price of this identical table is elevated to ‚Çπ1.8X. Following this adjustment, a special concession of 30% is applied to the newly established marked price. The inquiry then seeks to ascertain the resulting percentage profit generated by the store during this specific celebratory sales period. What is the percentage profit earned by the store during the festive sale ?

A software company developed 1600 licenses for a new application at a total cost of ‚Çπ8,000,000. They provided 100 licenses free to their top beta testers. For the remaining licenses, they offered a 30% discount on the market price of ‚Çπ12000 per license. Additionally, they gave 1 license free for every 9 licenses bought. If all 1600 licenses were distributed, what is the company's overall gain or loss percentage?

A company produced 6000 chocolates at a total cost of ‚Çπ180,000. They distributed 600 chocolates for free as samples. For the remaining chocolates, they offered a 10% discount on the market price of ‚Çπ40 per chocolate. Additionally, they gave 1 chocolate free with every 19 chocolates sold. If all 6000 chocolates were distributed, what is their overall profit or loss percentage ?

A wholesaler sells a packet of tea to a retailer at a profit of 25%. The retailer then marks up the price by 40% and offers a discount of 10% to the customer. If the customer pays Rs. 630 , find the cost price of the packet for the wholesaler.

An electronics dealer marks an item 50% above cost and offers a festival discount of 20%, plus a cashback of Rs. 200 on the final selling price. If he still makes a 25% profit, what is the cost price ?
A person bought a cycle at a discount of 25% on the marked price. He spent Rs. 510 on repairs and sold it for Rs. 5760, gaining 20%. What was the marked price?
A seller offers a 30% discount on an item whose cost price is Rs. 800. Despite the discount, the seller earns a profit of 25%. What must be the approx marked price?
A computer is sold at Rs. 6800 after applying a 15% discount. What was its market price ?

A store announces a festive deal: "Get 20% off on purchases above Rs. 15,000, and an additional Rs. 2,000 off if the total after discount exceeds Rs. 20,000." A customer buys items worth Rs. 40,000. How much does the customer finally pay ?

A wholesaler offers a bulk order deal where a 20% discount is applied to orders above Rs. 10,000, and a 5% additional seasonal discount is available. What is the effective price paid for an order worth Rs. 50,000 ?
A trader gave a 25 % discount on the market price but still earned a 15 % profit. What percentage was the market price above the cost price ?
A vendor offers a 10% discount on the marked price and sells a packet of salty biscuits for ‚Çπ180. If the profit earned on this sale is 50%, find the cost price.

A distributor marks a batch of 220 identical mobile chargers at 45% above the cost price and offers a 15% discount to retailers. A retailer purchases all the chargers and spends ‚Çπ14,790. What is the cost price per charger for the wholesaler ?
A seller gives a 10% discount and charges 10% GST on the discounted price. On a Rs. 5000 item, how much is payable?
A shopkeeper offers a festival deal, "Flat Rs. 175 off on every purchase above Rs. 1100." What is the effective discount percentage if a buyer purchases an item marked Rs. 1400?
If the price tag of a microwave is Rs. 4500 and the shopkeeper offers a discount of 19%, what is the selling price?

A departmental store marked up the price of an electronic gadget by 40% above its cost price. During a clearance sale, it offered two successive discounts of 20% and 10%. If the final selling price was Rs. 2,520 what was the cost price of the gadget ?
A buyer gets two successive discounts of 20% and 10% on a Rs. 800 item, What is the price paid?
A trader marks his goods 20% above the cost price and gives a discount of 10 %. If he gains Rs. 72 on an item, what is the cost price of the item?
An online shopkeeper offers a Rs. 180 discount on an item originally priced at Rs. 3600 . What percentage discount is being offered ?
A shopkeeper marks an article at Rs. 360 and gives a discount of Rs. 72 . He earns a profit of Rs. 18 . What is the cost price ?
A store offers a "50% off" discount but charges 10% VAT on the discounted price. If the marked price is Rs. 3200, what is the final amount paid ?

A wholesaler sells a packet of tea to a retailer at a profit of 20%. The retailer then marks up the price by 35% and offers a discount of 16.67% to the customer. If the customer pays Rs. 864 , find the cost price of the packet for the wholesaler.

A distributor marks a batch of 280 identical mobile chargers at 60% above the cost price and offers a 20% discount to retailers. A retailer purchases all the chargers and spends ‚Çπ15,360. What is the cost price per charger for the wholesaler ?
A brand offers two successive discounts on a product: the first is 20%, and the second is 5%. What is the single equivalent discount percentage?
A trader allows a 20% discount on the marked price and still makes a profit of 25%. If the cost price of the article is ‚Çπ640, what is the marked price?
If the marked price of an item is ‚Çπ2,500 and the selling price is ‚Çπ2,200, what is the percentage discount ?
A sofa is marked at ‚Çπ6,000 and sold for ‚Çπ4,590 after two successive discounts of 15% and a certain percentage. What is the second discount?
A cycle is marked at ‚Çπ5,000 and sold for ‚Çπ4,600. What is the percentage discount?
A shopkeeper sells a shirt at 25% discount and still makes a 20% profit. What is the cost price if the marked price is ‚Çπ2,400?
A shopkeeper gives two successive discounts of 15% and 20%. What is the net discount percentage?
A shopkeeper allows two successive discounts of 20% and 25% on an article marked at ‚Çπ4,000. If he still earns a profit of 20%, what is the cost price of the article?
A product is marked at ‚Çπ900 with a 30% discount. What is the selling price?
A product priced at ‚Çπ750 is sold for ‚Çπ450. What is the percentage of discount offered?
Trader marks an item 40% above cost price and offers a 25% discount on the marked price. If the customer pays ‚Çπ840, what is the cost price of the item?
A product has a marked price of ‚Çπ1,500. If a 10% discount is applied, what is the selling price?
A person bought an article at a discount of 10% and sold it at 20% above the marked price. What is the profit percentage?
A Jacket is on sale for 25% off its original price of Rs 800. How much will you pay for the Jacket ?
A laptop marked at ‚Çπ20,000 is sold for ‚Çπ17,500. Find the discount percentage.
The marked price of a shoes is ‚Çπ800. A customer gets two successive discounts of 15% and 10%. What is the final price paid?
The marked price of a Freezer is ‚Çπ12,000. A customer is offered two successive discounts of 25% and 15%. What is the total discount received?
A table was sold for ‚Çπ2400 after giving a discount of 20% on the marked price. What was the marked price ?
A cooler is marked at ‚Çπ5,000. The shopkeeper gives a discount of 15% and still makes a profit of 25%. What is the cost price of the fan?
A shopkeeper is offering a 25% discount on a Mixer that originally costs Rs. 600. What is the sale price of the Mixer?
A shopkeeper marks an article at ‚Çπ1500 and offers a discount of 20%, but still makes a profit of 20%. What is the cost price?

A shopkeeper marks the price of a shirt as ‚Çπ1500. He gives a 20% discount on the marked price. To bring the final price down to ‚Çπ960, how much additional discount (on the reduced price) should he give?
A toy car is marked at Rs. 2,000 and is available at a 10% discount. What is the selling price ?
Find the net discount percentage for successive discounts of 10% and 50%.
A dealer buys a product for Rs. 12,000 and sells it at a gain of 20% after giving 10% discount. What is the marked price ?
The marked price of an article is ‚Çπ2,800. It is sold at three successive discounts of 15%, 8% and 5%. Find the selling price of the article (in ‚Çπ).
The price of an electronic machine decreased by 5%, 10% and 15% during the first three years. What will be the price of the machine after three years if the value at the beginning was ‚Çπ 8,000?

In view of Diwali, Rajnee, an electronics shop owner, offers a discount scheme to her customers that on buying 15 decorative bulbs, they get 5 decorative bulbs free. What is the effective percentage discount given by Rajnee to her customers ?

A shopkeeper declares the following three schemes of discounts. Which scheme is most beneficial to a customer ? A. Buy 25 items and get 30 of the same items B. Two successive discounts of 12% and 18% C. Two successive equal discounts of 15%

A trader sells 25 kg of rice at ‚Çπ1,400. A customer asks for 15% discount. The shopkeeper agrees to it but instead of 1 kg he gives 10% less rice. What is the effective discount that the customer gets (correct to one decimal place) ?
A shopkeeper marks his goods k% more than their cost price. If the shopkeeper allows a 20% discount and still gains 25%, then find the value of k.

A shopkeeper sells a notebook that has a marked price of ‚Çπ80 at a discount of 8% and gives a pen costing ‚Çπ8.60 free with each notebook. Even then he makes a profit of 17%. Find the cost price of each note book correct to two places of decimals.
A shopkeeper offers buy three, get one free; what is the percentage discount being offered by the shopkeeper in this scheme ?

A television costs ‚Çπ20,500 for a trader. He fixes its marked price as ‚Çπ24,000 and gives a discount of 15%. He further gives a discount of 5% for cash payment. Find his percentage of profit or loss.

A shopkeeper offered following four different discount schemes for buyers on an item. A. Two successive discounts of 8% B. A discount of 11% followed by a discount of 5%. C. Successive discounts of 13% and 3% D. A discount of 16%. If Rajiv wants to purchase the item from this shop at minimum price, he should opt for the discount scheme:

A retailer announces a discount of 25% for selling a mobile phone marked at ‚Çπ48,000. The cost price of the mobile phone is 45% below the marked price. He offers a further discount of 7.5% if the buyer returns his old mobile phone. What is the ratio of the profit percentages of the retailer with the return of the old mobile phone scheme to that of without the return of the old mobile phone, respectively ?
A shop has two promotional offers: buy three shirts, get one shirt free, or a 25% discount on the marked price. If a customer buys five using both offers, what discount will he get all together ?
An article is marked at ‚Çπ550. If it is sold at a discount of 40%, then the selling price becomes 10% more than its cost price. What is the cost price (in ‚Çπ) ?
In a clearance sale, a sari whose marked price was ‚Çπ10,490 is now sold for ‚Çπ9,441. What is the discount percentage on the sari ?
If, on a marked price, the difference between selling prices with a discount of 65% and with two successive discounts of 30% and 40% is ‚Çπ105, then the marked price (in ‚Çπ) is:
A shopkeeper allows a discount of 20% on the marked price of an item and thus gains 10%. What is the ratio of the cost price to the marked price of the item

A shopkeeper, on the eve of Diwali, allowed a series of discounts on television sets. Find the selling price of a television set, if the marked price of television is ‚Çπ1,200 and successive discounts are 15% and 10%.
A shopkeeper marked an article at ‚Çπ5,000. The shopkeeper allows successive discounts of 20%,15% and 10%. The selling price of the article is:
The difference between a discount of 25% and two successive discounts of 15% and 20% on a certain bill was ‚Çπ28. Find the amount of the bill.

A manufacturer announces a trade discount of 30% on his products for his dealers. One of the dealers purchased 6 smart phones manufactured by the company at the rate of Rs.25,000 each. What is the amount of trade discount?

A shopkeeper offers the following discount schemes for buyers. I. Two successive discounts of 15% and 20% II. Successive discount of 25 % and 10% III. A discount of 33% The selling price will be minimum under the scheme:
After offering two successive discounts a toy with a marked price of ‚Çπ150 is sold at ‚Çπ105. If the value of the first discount is 12.5%, what is the value of the second discount ?
If the selling price of an article is ‚Çπ1,792 after a 20% discount, then the marked price of the article is:
An article marked for ‚Çπ275 is sold at 5% discount. The seller still gains 4.5%. What did he pay for it ?
A cooker is marked at ‚Çπ12,500. The dealer allows successive discounts of 6%, 6% and 4% on it. What is the net selling price of the cooker ?
